  ],
  "text": "HTTP filter chain registration and execution in Envoy proxy follows a multi-stage flow:\n\n## Registration Stage\n\n**1. Configuration Definition (Proto)**\nThe HttpConnectionManager protobuf schema (envoyproxy/envoy api/envoy/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/v3/http_connection_manager.proto and envoyproxy/data-plane-api envoy/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/v3/http_connection_manager.proto) defines the filter_chain configuration with the http_filters repeated field containing HttpFilter messages. Each HttpFilter has a name, config_type (typed_config or config_discovery), is_optional flag, and disabled flag.\n\n**2. Filter Factory Registration (Config Phase)**\nHttpConnectionManagerConfig (envoyproxy/envoy source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h and .cc) implements the FilterChainFactory interface and is responsible for parsing the HttpConnectionManager configuration. During construction, it uses FilterChainHelper (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h) to process each filter configuration. The FilterChainHelper::processFilters method iterates through the http_filters, validates each filter through the factory registry, and creates FilterFactoryCb callback functions that encapsulate the filter creation logic. These callbacks are stored in filter_factories_ list as FilterFactoryProvider objects.\n\n**3. Filter Chain Construction (Connection Creation)**\nWhen a new HTTP stream is created, FilterChainFactory::createFilterChain (envoyproxy/envoy source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h) is called with a FilterChainManager reference. This method calls FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.cc) which iterates through the prepared filter factories and invokes each FilterFactoryCb via FilterChainManager::applyFilterFactoryCb.\n\n## Execution Stage\n\n**1. Filter Chain Manager Implementation**\nFilterChainManager is an interface (envoyproxy/envoy envoy/http/filter_factory.h) that defines how filters are added to the chain through applyFilterFactoryCb method. FilterManager (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h) implements both FilterChainManager and FilterChainFactoryCallbacks interfaces. When applyFilterFactoryCb is called, it invokes the FilterFactoryCb function passing itself as FilterChainFactoryCallbacks, allowing the factory to call addStreamDecoderFilter and addStreamEncoderFilter to register filters in the chain.\n\n**2. Active Filter Wrapping**\nEach filter added to the chain is wrapped in an ActiveStreamDecoderFilter or ActiveStreamEncoderFilter (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h) which manages filter state and iteration logic through ActiveStreamFilterBase (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h). These active wrappers maintain iteration state (Continue, StopSingleIteration, StopAllBuffer, StopAllWatermark) and handle the control flow based on filter callback return values.\n\n**3. Runtime Execution**\nAt runtime, FilterManager maintains two filter chains: decoder filters and encoder filters (stored in std::list containers). When HTTP headers arrive, FilterManager::decodeHeaders iterates through decoder filters in sequence, calling each filter's decodeHeaders method. The return value (FilterHeadersStatus) determines iteration behavior:\n- FilterHeadersStatus::Continue: Move to next filter\n- FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration: Stop iteration\n- FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer: Stop all iteration and buffer data\n\nSimilarly, FilterManager::decodeData and FilterManager::decodeTrailers process request body and trailer data. The commonContinue method (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.cc) handles resumption of iteration when filters call continueDecoding().\n\nFor response path, FilterManager::encodeHeaders, FilterManager::encodeData, and FilterManager::encodeTrailers process response headers, body, and trailers through registered encoder filters following the same iteration mechanism.\n\n**4. Dynamic Configuration (ECDS)**\nThe system supports dynamic filter configuration through ExtensionConfigDiscoveryService (ECDS). FilterChainHelper can process filters with config_discovery instead of typed_config, creating dynamic filter config providers that can update filter configurations at runtime without requiring proxy reloads.\n\n## Key Design Patterns\n\n**Configuration-Time vs Runtime-Time Separation:** Filter factory callbacks (FilterFactoryCb) are created once during configuration initialization, then reused for each new stream. This enables efficient filter instantiation.\n\n**Visitor Pattern:** FilterChainFactoryCallbacks serves as a visitor interface that filter factories use to add filters to the chain, decoupling filter creation logic from chain construction.\n\n**State Machine:** ActiveStreamFilterBase maintains an IterationState enum that tracks whether iteration should continue, stop for single frames, or stop for all frames with optional buffering.\n\n**Dual Path Architecture:** The system maintains separate decoder and encoder filter chains to process bidirectional HTTP traffic (request/response), with the router filter serving as the terminal bridge between the two paths."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Envoy HTTP Filter Chain Registration and Execution

## Your Task

Trace how HTTP filter chains are registered and executed in the Envoy proxy. Find C++ and protobuf files across envoyproxy/envoy and envoyproxy/data-plane-api that implement: the FilterChainFactory interface (in envoy/http/), the FilterChainHelper that constructs chains from config, the FilterManager that executes filter chains at runtime, and the HttpConnectionManager proto that defines the filter_chain configuration schema.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossrepo tracing domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you trace the HTTP filter chain registration and execution in Envoy. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and then systematically find the relevant components.
Now let me create a comprehensive answer by examining a few more key files to ensure completeness:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive trace of the HTTP filter chain registration and execution in Envoy proxy. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Components Identified

**Registration & Configuration:**
- **FilterChainFactory** (envoy/http/filter_factory.h) - Interface for creating HTTP filter chains
- **FilterChainManager** (envoy/http/filter_factory.h) - Interface for adding filters to chains
- **FilterChainHelper** (source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h) - Processes filter configurations from protobuf
- **FilterChainUtility** (source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.cc) - Constructs filter chains from configuration
- **HttpConnectionManagerConfig** (source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h) - Implements FilterChainFactory

**Execution & Runtime:**
- **FilterManager** (source/common/http/filter_manager.h) - Implements FilterChainManager and manages filter execution
- **DownstreamFilterManager** (source/common/http/filter_manager.h) - Specialized FilterManager for downstream
- **ActiveStreamFilterBase** (source/common/http/filter_manager.h) - Base class for wrapping individual filters with state management
- **ActiveStreamDecoderFilter/ActiveStreamEncoderFilter** (source/common/http/filter_manager.h) - Wraps decoder/encoder filters

**Configuration Schema:**
- **HttpConnectionManager** protobuf in both envoyproxy/envoy and envoyproxy/data-plane-api with http_filters field defining filter chains
- **HttpFilter** message defining individual filter configuration with support for typed_config and dynamic config_discovery

## Execution Flow

1. Configuration phase: FilterChainHelper processes HttpConnectionManager proto and creates FilterFactoryCb functions
2. Stream creation: FilterChainFactory::createFilterChain is called, which uses FilterChainUtility to invoke filter factories
3. Filter instantiation: Each FilterFactoryCb registers filters via FilterChainManager::applyFilterFactoryCb
4. Runtime: FilterManager maintains decoder/encoder filter chains and iterates through them based on return statuses

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbols, execution chain, and detailed narrative explanation.

  "text": "HTTP filter chain registration and execution in Envoy proxy follows a multi-stage flow:\n\n## Registration Stage\n\n**1. Configuration Definition (Proto)**\nThe HttpConnectionManager protobuf schema (envoyproxy/envoy api/envoy/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/v3/http_connection_manager.proto and envoyproxy/data-plane-api envoy/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/v3/http_connection_manager.proto) defines the filter_chain configuration with the http_filters repeated field containing HttpFilter messages. Each HttpFilter has a name, config_type (typed_config or config_discovery), is_optional flag, and disabled flag.\n\n**2. Filter Factory Registration (Config Phase)**\nHttpConnectionManagerConfig (envoyproxy/envoy source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h and .cc) implements the FilterChainFactory interface and is responsible for parsing the HttpConnectionManager configuration. During construction, it uses FilterChainHelper (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h) to process each filter configuration. These callbacks are stored in filter_factories_ list as FilterFactoryProvider objects.\n\n**3. Filter Chain Construction (Connection Creation)**\nWhen a new HTTP stream is created, FilterChainFactory::createFilterChain (envoyproxy/envoy source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h) is called with a FilterChainManager reference. Filter Chain Manager Implementation**\nFilterChainManager is an interface (envoyproxy/envoy envoy/http/filter_factory.h) that defines how filters are added to the chain through applyFilterFactoryCb method. FilterManager (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h) implements both FilterChainManager and FilterChainFactoryCallbacks interfaces. When applyFilterFactoryCb is called, it invokes the FilterFactoryCb function passing itself as FilterChainFactoryCallbacks, allowing the factory to call addStreamDecoderFilter and addStreamEncoderFilter to register filters in the chain.\n\n**2. Active Filter Wrapping**\nEach filter added to the chain is wrapped in an ActiveStreamDecoderFilter or ActiveStreamEncoderFilter (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h) which manages filter state and iteration logic through ActiveStreamFilterBase (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.h). Runtime Execution**\nAt runtime, FilterManager maintains two filter chains: decoder filters and encoder filters (stored in std::list containers). When HTTP headers arrive, FilterManager::decodeHeaders iterates through decoder filters in sequence, calling each filter's decodeHeaders method. The commonContinue method (envoyproxy/envoy source/common/http/filter_manager.cc) handles resumption of iteration when filters call continueDecoding().\n\nFor response path, FilterManager::encodeHeaders, FilterManager::encodeData, and FilterManager::encodeTrailers process response headers, body, and trailers through registered encoder filters following the same iteration mechanism.\n\n**4. Dynamic Configuration (ECDS)**\nThe system supports dynamic filter configuration through ExtensionConfigDiscoveryService (ECDS). FilterChainHelper can process filters with config_discovery instead of typed_config, creating dynamic filter config providers that can update filter configurations at runtime without requiring proxy reloads.\n\n## Key Design Patterns\n\n**Configuration-Time vs Runtime-Time Separation:** Filter factory callbacks (FilterFactoryCb) are created once during configuration initialization, then reused for each new stream. }
